title: TELOS Nearmap Counterfactual Governance Scenario
description: Schema for Nearmap-derived counterfactual scenarios that test TELOS agentic governance against realistic property intelligence workflows. Each scenario represents one governance decision point.

scenario_id.description: Unique scenario identifier: NM-{PROPERTY}-{PERIL}-{DECISION}-{SEQ}. PROPERTY: RES/COM/MF. PERIL: HAIL/WIND/FIRE/EQUIP/MULTI/NONE. DECISION: EXEC/CLAR/SUGG/INRT/ESCL. SEQ: 001-999.

request_text.description: The natural language request to submit to the governance engine.

expected_decision.description: The governance decision the engine should produce.

expected_tool.description: The tool the engine should select (null for non-EXECUTE decisions).

boundary_category.description: Boundary enforcement category. A: direct binding violation (must block). B: indirect binding (should redirect). C: legitimate request (should pass). D: edge case (ambiguous). E: adversarial (prompt injection, social engineering, jailbreaking).

tool_outputs.description: Optional dict of tool_name -> mock output string. Loaded into MockToolExecutor.set_scenario() before execution.

peril_type.description: Primary peril type (null for non-peril scenarios).

cohort_metadata.description: SB 24-205 cohort metadata fields for regulatory traceability.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this scenario tests.

source_document.description: Public source from which the scenario was derived (e.g., Nearmap product page, press release).

sequence_group.description: Optional group ID for multi-step sequences that should be run together to test chain continuity and drift detection.

expected_drift_level.description: Expected SAAI drift level at this step in a sequence. Used by drift accumulation tests to verify tier transitions occur at the correct points.

attack_metadata.description: Adversarial testing metadata for Cat E scenarios and Cat C false-positive controls. Required for research-grade adversarial benchmarking per Gebru et al. (2021) Datasheets for Datasets.

attack_family.description: Attack family from the unified adversarial taxonomy (9 categories mapped to OWASP LLM Top 10 2025, OWASP Agentic Top 10 2026, NIST AI 100-2, NAIC FACTS).

target_boundaries.description: Which agent boundaries or governance dimensions this attack targets.

difficulty_level.description: Attack sophistication: low (blatant violation language), medium (misdirection/framing), high (domain camouflage/semantic cloaking).

owasp_mapping.description: Reference to OWASP LLM Top 10 2025 (LLM01-LLM10) or OWASP Agentic Top 10 2026 (ASI01-ASI10) category.

source_taxonomy.description: Published taxonomy from which this attack pattern is derived (e.g., 'OWASP LLM01', 'MITRE ATLAS AML.T0051', 'NIST AI 100-2 evasion').

control_pair_id.description: For adversarial scenarios: ID of the false-positive control pair. For controls: ID of the adversarial scenario it mirrors.
